what_it_does: Records a mobile UI/UX analysis of the gallery viewer, combining Tim's stated findings, agent-found findings, evidence, root causes, rule/token-based directions, resolved design decisions, and remaining sequencing questions.
observations: Frames itself as analysis-only loop preparation, not an implemented redesign or binding spec.
Uses Tim's screenshot, live mobile measurements, and file/line root-cause notes as its evidence base.
Centers the synthesis that mobile chrome dominates the work surface and that the slide is visually demoted.
Separates Tim's findings from agent-found findings, then consolidates them into rule/token-core opportunities.
Investigates the difference between Review, Feedback, and My notes, then proposes LOOK, MARK, and READ BACK as clearer verbs.
Records Tim's later decisions about landscape layouts, zoom, panel swapping, and folding the skim strip into a scrubber.
Crystallizes the mobile viewer as WORK | PANEL slots with split-ratio, scale, panel-fill, and traverse-scrubber concepts.
standouts: The document ties observed UI issues to concrete measurements and stated root causes rather than only describing impressions.
It treats the redesign as a rule/token-core problem as well as a mobile usability problem.
The resolved direction unifies landscape layout, fullscreen, zoom, panel swapping, comparison, and navigation under slot, dial, fill, and traverse concepts.
conventions: Uses numbered sections with short codes for findings: T1-T8 for Tim's findings and A1-A7 for agent-found findings.
Each finding generally follows an evidence, root cause, and direction pattern.
Maintains distinction between analysis, tentative direction, resolved decisions, and open questions.
Uses emphatic uppercase labels and bold phrases to mark status and conceptual pivots.
Mixes measured UI facts, file-local implementation references, quoted user language, and inferred design directions in one narrative document.
concerns: The open questions section says only the sequencing question remains, but still lists earlier questions that were marked answered above.
Several directions depend on named system concepts mentioned in the document, such as DNA, faces, dials, slots, and Packet 01, without defining them fully in this file.
Some file and line references are cited as evidence, but the referenced code is not included in this file.
notes: Brief this file as the recorded mobile gallery viewer investigation that turns observed UI clashes into rule-based viewer primitives and unresolved sequencing questions.
questions: Should the bottom rail or another affordance host the consolidated faces/tools after the panel model is adopted?
Which of the listed open questions are still active after the resolved decisions in section 5.1?
What exact build campaign or sequencing decision followed this analysis-only record?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the actual content: it records an analysis-only investigation, evidence, directions, resolved decisions, and remaining questions without presenting itself as implemented work.
apparent_themes: typed marks over piece or element targets
slotted composition with dials and refillable panels
navigation as traverse across reading axes
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to preserve a detailed diagnostic record and convert mobile UI complaints into reusable rule-system concepts before any redesign build begins.
novelty: The file treats a mobile gallery redesign as an instance of the system's own design grammar, using slots, dials, fills, reading axes, marks, and token proofs as analysis outputs.
contribution: Contributes a measured mobile-gallery diagnosis, a vocabulary for separating inspect, record, and readback actions, and a neutral set of viewer primitives: WORK | PANEL slots, split ratio, work scale, refillable panel, and traverse scrubber.
summary_for_embedding: Analysis record for the mobile gallery viewer that documents screenshot/live-measurement findings, root causes, rule/token directions, Tim's resolved decisions, and a proposed WORK | PANEL viewer model.
intention_for_embedding: Prepared to brief and seed a future rule-based mobile redesign campaign without implementing the redesign in this file.
